title: Scan tracked project files without ignored environment noise

sources.reason: The Doctor currently uses unrestricted Path.glob and can inspect ignored local environments.
The cockpit doctor should infer template maintenance mode from the confirmed template profile.
Readiness helpers need an explicit execution-mode override that preserves environment behavior for existing callers.
Regression coverage for ignored environment exclusion and tracked evidence.
Regression coverage for explicit template maintenance execution mode.
Regression coverage for automatic template maintenance context in cockpit-doctor.
Bandit low-risk evidence must be regenerated after adding subprocess-based repository scanning.
Regression coverage for cockpit-doctor template maintenance messaging.
This contract declares the Doctor scanning and role inference scope.

riskAssessment.reason: The change narrows read-only evidence discovery and preserves non-mutating Doctor output.

executionDecision.reason: Tracked-file scanning and template-role messaging are implementable with local regression tests.

preReviewWarnings: Review Git fallback behavior for non-repository fixtures and ensure ignored directories never become evidence in repository mode.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Project Doctor uses tracked files as its default evidence source inside Git repositories.
Ignored directories such as .venv, target, build, and caches do not create language or framework evidence.
Non-Git temporary fixtures retain a deterministic filesystem fallback for unit tests.
cockpit-doctor automatically uses template maintenance context when repositoryRole is template and template evidence is complete.
Regression tests cover ignored-file noise, tracked evidence, and template maintenance messaging.
guidelines: Doctor remains read-only and does not modify project profile or Guard approvals.
Evidence scanning must be deterministic and independent of the agent's current virtual environment.
Existing non-Git fixture behavior remains supported by an explicit fallback.

intent.problem: Project Doctor scans the entire filesystem under the repository root, so ignored .venv site-packages can be misclassified as project languages and template repositories can receive adopted-project warnings.
constraints: Do not make ignored local environments part of Repository Truth.
intent.rationale: Evidence discovery should reflect repository-owned facts while still allowing isolated unit fixtures that do not have a Git index.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: ignored .venv JavaScript does not become project language evidence
non-Git temporary fixture still detects explicit source files
confirmed template maintenance avoids adopted-project warning

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized the accepted Doctor remediation; the Bandit baseline is regenerated by the repository check after the subprocess scanner change.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
